sections.paragraph:
ἔνταλμα, ατος, τό (s. ἐντέλλω; LXX; PLond IV, 1384, 55 [VIII a.d.]; Just., D. 46, 5; 67, 10) that which is commanded as officially binding, commandment (w. διδασκαλία) ἐ. ἀνθρώπων Mt 15:9; Mk 7:7; Col 2:22 (all three Is 29:13); τὰ τοῦ κυρίου ἐ. 2 Cl 17:3.—DELG s.v. τέλλω.

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

2 Cl 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 339
